preamble: The change from the voiceless labiodental fricative /f/ to the palatal glide /j/ is a rare phonological process. It involves a shift in both place and manner of articulation, moving from labiodental to palatal and from a fricative to an approximant. This may occur as an extreme form of palatalization before a high front vowel.
sources: Ladefoged, P., & Maddieson, I. (1996). The Sounds of the World's Languages.
Wells, J. C. (1982). Accents of English.
